name_en: Supreme Council of the Cultural Revolution
description: The Supreme Council of the Cultural Revolution is an Iranian state body created in December 1984 to succeed the original seven-member Cultural Revolution Committee (also known as the Cultural Revolution Headquarters) that [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) established in June 1980. The original committee was charged with closing universities, purging them of Western and secular influences, and reshaping academic curricula along Islamic lines during the [Iranian Cultural Revolution](/events/iranian-cultural-revolution-1980). The council continues to set cultural and educational policy for the Islamic Republic, including oversight of universities, regulation of the internet, and media policy.
